mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates compress equity discount rates, directly supporting higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels or changes over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9 percent signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a rank IC above 0.05 over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above 95.0 confirms manufacturing sector stabilization, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 returns as supply-side constraints ease.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above 95.0 are followed by negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations during the early recovery phase.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and a declining unemployment rate signals effective monetary transmission to the real economy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of low FEDFUNDS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDP growth directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 100 thousand signal robust labor market expansion that boosts consumer confidence and equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases above 100 thousand are followed by negative 20-day returns in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation readings alongside persistently low policy rates trigger expectations of premature monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a rank IC below -0.03 over a rolling 3-year window.
